titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Dickins family papers
titlestmt.author: by Emily Komornik

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0 3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 05:00

did.unittitle: Dickins family papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Dickins Family Papers contains correspondence, writings, legal documents, and other papers relating to Rachel Dickins, Thomas Eden Dickins, and other members of the Dickins family, as well as diaries, poetry, and a collection of fifteenth through seventeenth century charters with seals. Other materials in the collection relate to James Bean, an assistant paymaster with the East India Company in the Philippines in 1764, Captain William Wilkinson, a financial agent to Sir George Hay, and Wallace of Craigie, a descendant of William Wallace. Also included are genealogical charts linking the Dickins family to Pocahontas through her marriage to John Rolfe, and twentieth century documents concerning family history.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bernard Quaritch on the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2004.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1795-1928 and undated. II. Charters and Legal Documents, circa 1449-1670. III. Other Papers, 1725-2000 and undated.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Dickins family lived in Oxfordshire and Warwickshire, England, and included Rachel Dickins (-1761) of Suffolk; Thomas Eden Dickins (1832-1857), 2nd Lieutenant of Artillery in the East India Company; Colonel Francis E.A. Dickins (1827-1912), a merchant in India; Reverend Charles Dickins (1829-1917); William Dickins (1795-1885), a barrister; and W.P. Dickins (active 1899).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Dickins Family Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Dickins Family Papers contains correspondence, writings, legal documents, and other papers relating to Rachel Dickins, Thomas Eden Dickins, and other members of the Dickins family, as well as diaries, poetry, and a collection of fifteenth through seventeenth century charters with seals. Other materials in the collection relate to James Bean, an assistant paymaster with the East India Company in the Philippines in 1764, Captain William Wilkinson, a financial agent to Sir George Hay, and Wallace of Craigie, a descendant of William Wallace. Also included are genealogical charts linking the Dickins family to Pocahontas through her marriage to John Rolfe, and twentieth century documents concerning family history.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Dickins Family Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

geogname.part: Great Britain -- Colonies -- Description and travel

did.unittitle: Correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Dickins Family, Ellis Family, and Other Correspondence. Materials within each grouping maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence between members of the Dickins family, the Ellis family, and others, including Thomas Hay of Mordington and Captain William Wilkinson. Included are letters from Francis Atherton Dickins to his wife, Emily Elwes Dickins, during a six-month tour of Kashmir, India, as a trader with the East India Company, letters to Lucy Park Dickins from her father, J.A. Park and her husband, solicitor William Dickins; and letters concerning financial transactions, especially loans, between Thomas Hay and Captain William Wilkinson.

did.unittitle: Dickins Family
Herbert S. Dickins letters to S. E. [Nina] Dickins
J.A. Park and family to daughter, Lucy Dickins
William Dickins, solicitor, to his wife Lucy Park [Dickins]
Reverend Charles Dickins to Miss S.E. Dickins
Miscellaneous Dickins family
Letters to Miss Selvia Dickins, 1849-1890
Letters from Thomas Eden Dickins to various correspondents
Colonel Francis Atherton Dickins to his wife, Emily Elwes Dickins
Ellis Family
Other Correspondence
General
Thomas Hay of Mordington and Captain William Wilkinson
Unsigned letter from Gloucester Castle [with transcription]
Charters and Legal Documents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains English, Welsh, and Scottish charters, indentures, and exemplifications, mostly relating to property. Documents are chiefly written in Latin on vellum and affixed with wax seals of varying sizes.

did.unittitle: Welsh title deeds
[English] exemplification
Scottish charters with seals
Documents relating to Wallace of Craigie
Welsh charter
Indenture
Charter relating to John Martland, Second Earl of Lauerdale
Other Papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily contains twentieth century materials related to family history, such as correspondence, legal documents, printed materials, transcriptions, and dealer inventories, as well as eighteenth and nineteenth century writings, including poetry, diaries, and the last will and testament of Rachel Dickins. Genealogical charts and family seats track the history of the Dickins family and connect them to Pocahontas (Matoaka) through her marriage to John Rolfe. Printed materials include brochures and pamphlets describing Dickins family property, including the houses in Chastleton and Cherington, as well as a history of the Craigie family.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Dickins family pedigree and A History of Chastleton
Pocahontas collection [genealogical charts and biographical printouts]
Chastleton House and correspondence regarding the Dickins collection
Dickins family history
Dickins family pedigree, genealogy charts, and accounts
Eight poems by J.C.P.
Catalog and collection of historical manuscripts and autograph letters
Leather notebook with poem referring to Napoleon's campaign in Egypt
Handwritten description of some items [possibly by dealer]
Hampstead literature
Payne, Major P.I.C. [letter and supporting documentation regarding James Bean's involvement with the East India Company]
Payne Major P.I.C. [letter and transcription from the National Army Museum]
"Conservationist's Guide to Hampstead"
Book of Wallace, vol. I [printout, annotated]
[Letter from G.I. Smiley, Clerk of the Somerset County Council, regarding public records of the Parish Council]
Civil War commission [including an attestation and release of debts by Thomas Hay of Mordington and letters from Captain William Wilkinson]
Invitation to Walton Bridge Regatta and supporting documents
Rachel Dickins' recipe and receipt book
"Probate of the Will of Rachel Dickins, Widow" [and supporting documents]
"Six Months in Fairyland" [journal of Colonel F.A. Dickins]
Dickins family history
Dickins family history and family works
A Little History of Cherington and Stourton, Warwickshire , by Margaret Dickins [printout]
Copy of a letter from the retired Reverend Bishop Jebb to a clergyman and his diocese
Copper engraved billheads, [with notes, invoices, and some correspondence]
